hotspots.title: Trump administration’s Iran war costs and strategies
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration faces intense scrutiny over the financial and human costs of the ongoing conflict with Iran, with rising gas prices and troop deployments sparking public and congressional backlash. The 60-day War Powers deadline has passed without clear resolution, fueling debates over executive authority and military spending.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its high-stakes geopolitical implications, economic impact, and the administration's defiance of congressional oversight, creating a charged political environment.

hotspots.title: Magyar Péter’s nepotism and government formation
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar faces accusations of nepotism after appointing his brother-in-law as justice minister, while his party, Tisza, grapples with transparency issues over pre-election donations from billionaires. The controversy threatens to undermine Magyar’s promises of anti-corruption and reform.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the moral outrage over perceived cronyism, the contrast with Magyar’s reformist rhetoric, and the broader implications for Hungary’s democratic institutions.

hotspots.title: Rassemblement National’s Mâcon rally and 2027 ambitions
hotspots.blurb: France’s far-right Rassemblement National (RN) held a high-profile rally in Mâcon, with Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella addressing economic policies and the party’s 2027 presidential ambitions. The event underscored internal divisions over leadership and strategy ahead of a critical judicial ruling on Le Pen’s eligibility.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the high-stakes leadership dynamics, the party’s rising influence, and the charged rhetoric around nationalism and economic reform.

hotspots.title: Vox’s ‘national priority’ and regional pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s far-right Vox party continues to push its ‘national priority’ agenda, clashing with regional governments over immigration, housing, and trade policies. The party’s influence is growing in Andalusia and Castilla y León, where it negotiates pacts with the conservative PP amid accusations of extremism.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the polarizing nature of Vox’s policies, the high-stakes regional negotiations, and the broader implications for Spain’s political landscape.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s Nigel Farage and electoral surge
hotspots.blurb: Nigel Farage’s Reform UK party is gaining momentum ahead of local elections, with Farage himself campaigning aggressively across the UK. The party’s rise is fueled by discontent with the Conservative government, though controversies over donations and candidate conduct threaten to undermine its credibility.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to Farage’s polarizing persona, the party’s rapid electoral gains, and the charged rhetoric around immigration and economic reform.
